epigraph.text: Democracy is the theory that the common people know what they want, and deserve to get it good and hard.

follyOfTheDay.headline: Suburb Threatens Homeowner With $150K in Fines Over Mailbox Hue
follyOfTheDay.summary:
A suburban homeowner has fallen victim to the remorseless machinery of bureaucratic zealotry after painting his mailbox the objectively indistinguishable shade of hunter green instead of the mandated forest green. The HOA board, armed with bylaws and devoid of mercy, has commenced daily fines of $5,000—a sum that would bankrupt most families within a month, yet somehow remains justified by gentlemen in polo shirts who derive their only sense of power from the persecution of their neighbors.

The accused homeowner, caught between compliance and financial ruin, has become the latest martyr to the HOA industrial complex: that uniquely American institution wherein the ornamental acquisition of minor authority drives otherwise ordinary men to acts of bureaucratic sadism. In response, one might reasonably ask whether the distinction between these two shades of green is perceptible to the human eye, or merely to the diseased imagination of committee members seeking validation.
follyOfTheDay.commentary: A reminder that evil often wears a polo shirt and a laminated badge. The mailbox has won.
